title: Add OCI Image Spec annotations to container images
description:
**Size:** 🔧 small
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** medium
**Source:** Proposed for 'Currently no OCI labels are being set on the container'

Add standard OCI annotations to python-base, venv-builder, and keystone container images using a two-layer approach:

1. **CI-generated labels via `docker/metadata-action@v5`** (3 new steps in `.github/workflows/build-images.yaml`):
   - `meta-python-base` step in `build-base-images` job before `build-python-base` step, generating OCI labels (title, description, source, url, version, created, revision, licenses, vendor)
   - `meta-venv-builder` step in `build-base-images` job before `build-venv-builder` step, same label set
   - `meta-keystone` step in `build-service-images` job before `build-service` step, with `version` overridden to `${{ steps.source-ref.outputs.ref }}` (upstream release version from source-refs.yaml) via `raw` version strategy
   - Wire each metadata step's `labels` output into the corresponding `build-push-action` step via `labels: ${{ steps.<meta-step-id>.outputs.labels }}`

2. **Static `LABEL` instructions in Dockerfiles** (3 files):
   - Add `org.opencontainers.image.title`, `.description`, `.licenses=Apache-2.0`, `.vendor` as static LABELs to `images/python-base/Dockerfile`, `images/venv-builder/Dockerfile`, and `images/keystone/Dockerfile`
   - These provide baseline metadata for local builds; CI-generated labels override them at push time

Excluded: tag logic changes, custom/vendor-specific labels, base.name/base.digest annotations, Makefile changes, verify job changes.

**Rationale:** Container images currently ship with zero metadata labels. This makes it impossible to trace a running image back to its source commit, repository, license, or build timestamp without external tooling. OCI annotations are the industry standard adopted by registries (Docker Hub, GHCR, Quay), vulnerability scanners, and audit tools. Adding them improves supply chain transparency, simplifies incident response (which commit produced this image?), and aligns with container best practices at minimal cost.

**Affected Areas:**
- .github/workflows/build-images.yaml
- images/python-base/Dockerfile
- images/venv-builder/Dockerfile
- images/keystone/Dockerfile

stories.title: DevOps engineer inspects image provenance in container registry
stories.want: container images pushed to GHCR to carry standard OCI annotations (title, description, source URL, version, build timestamp, Git revision, license, vendor)
stories.so_that: I can inspect image provenance directly in the registry UI or via docker inspect without needing external tooling
criteria: GHCR displays org.opencontainers.image.title for each pushed image (python-base, venv-builder, keystone)
org.opencontainers.image.revision matches the Git SHA that triggered the build
org.opencontainers.image.created contains an ISO 8601 timestamp from the CI run
org.opencontainers.image.source points to the GitHub repository URL

stories.title: Developer identifies locally-built images via static labels
stories.want: locally-built container images to carry baseline OCI labels (title, description, license, vendor) even without the CI pipeline
stories.so_that: I can distinguish images and verify basic metadata during local development and testing
criteria: docker inspect on a locally-built python-base image shows org.opencontainers.image.title=python-base
docker inspect on a locally-built venv-builder image shows org.opencontainers.image.title=venv-builder
docker inspect on a locally-built keystone image shows org.opencontainers.image.title=keystone

stories.title: Security auditor verifies supply chain metadata on container images
stories.want: pushed container images to include source commit, license, and build timestamp annotations
stories.so_that: I can verify supply chain compliance and trace any image back to its source
criteria: org.opencontainers.image.revision is a 40-character Git SHA on every pushed image
org.opencontainers.image.licenses is Apache-2.0 on every image
org.opencontainers.image.vendor identifies the producing organization (SAP SE)
org.opencontainers.image.url provides a link to the project repository

stories.title: CI pipeline stamps keystone image with upstream release version
stories.want: the keystone image's org.opencontainers.image.version to reflect the upstream OpenStack release version from source-refs.yaml
stories.so_that: the OCI version annotation matches the actual software version (e.g., 28.0.0) rather than a Git-derived value
criteria: org.opencontainers.image.version on the keystone image equals the value from source-refs.yaml (e.g., 28.0.0)
The metadata-action step for keystone uses a raw version strategy with the source-ref output
python-base and venv-builder use the default Git-derived version from metadata-action
Version override does not affect existing tag derivation logic (composite/version/SHA tags unchanged)

requirements.description: All three Dockerfiles (python-base, venv-builder, keystone) SHALL contain static LABEL instructions for org.opencontainers.image.title, .description, .licenses, and .vendor
requirements.rationale: Static labels provide baseline OCI metadata for local builds where docker/metadata-action is not available. CI-generated labels override these at push time, but local builds still carry meaningful metadata.

scenarios.name: Local python-base build has static labels
scenarios.when: a developer runs docker build images/python-base -t python-base
and_then: org.opencontainers.image.vendor and org.opencontainers.image.description are also present

scenarios.name: Local keystone build has static labels in runtime stage only
scenarios.when: a developer builds keystone locally following the local build instructions
scenarios.then: docker inspect shows org.opencontainers.image.title=keystone with all four static label keys
and_then: labels are in the runtime stage (Stage 2) — the build stage labels are discarded

scenarios.name: CI-generated labels coexist with static labels
scenarios.when: the CI pipeline builds and pushes an image with metadata-action labels
scenarios.then: the pushed image carries both static labels (from Dockerfile) and CI-generated labels (from metadata-action)
and_then: CI-generated labels for dynamic values (created, revision, source) supplement the static labels

requirements.description: The build-images workflow SHALL include docker/metadata-action steps to generate OCI-compliant labels for python-base, venv-builder, and keystone images
requirements.rationale: docker/metadata-action automatically generates labels from GitHub Actions context (commit SHA, repository URL, timestamps) that cannot be determined statically in Dockerfiles. This is the industry-standard approach for CI-generated container metadata.

scenarios.name: build-base-images has metadata step for python-base
scenarios.then: a docker/metadata-action step with id meta-python-base runs before the build-python-base step
and_then: the step specifies images: ghcr.io/${{ steps.meta.outputs.owner }}/python-base

scenarios.name: build-base-images has metadata step for venv-builder
scenarios.then: a docker/metadata-action step with id meta-venv-builder runs before the build-venv-builder step
and_then: the step specifies images: ghcr.io/${{ steps.meta.outputs.owner }}/venv-builder

scenarios.name: build-service-images has metadata step for keystone
scenarios.then: a docker/metadata-action step with id meta-keystone runs before the build-service step
and_then: the step specifies images referencing the tags.outputs.image computed earlier

requirements.description: The keystone metadata-action step SHALL override org.opencontainers.image.version with the upstream release version from source-refs.yaml via the raw version strategy
requirements.rationale: The default metadata-action version derives from Git branch/tag, which does not represent the upstream OpenStack version. Using the raw strategy with steps.source-ref.outputs.ref ensures the OCI version annotation (e.g., 28.0.0) matches the actual software being packaged.

scenarios.name: Keystone version is upstream release version
scenarios.when: the meta-keystone step processes the tags input
scenarios.then: the tags input contains type=raw,value=${{ steps.source-ref.outputs.ref }}
and_then: the generated org.opencontainers.image.version label reflects the value from source-refs.yaml

scenarios.name: Base image versions use default strategy
scenarios.when: the meta-python-base or meta-venv-builder steps generate labels
scenarios.then: no tags input is specified (default Git-derived version)
and_then: base images do not reference source-refs.yaml for version

scenarios.name: Version override does not affect tag derivation
scenarios.when: the meta-keystone step overrides the version label
scenarios.then: the existing Derive tags step and its composite/version/SHA tag outputs remain unchanged
and_then: image tags are identical to pre-CC-0031 behavior

requirements.description: All docker/metadata-action uses SHALL be pinned to a full 40-character commit SHA with a version comment, following the project's action pinning convention
requirements.rationale: All existing actions in the workflow are pinned to SHAs with # vN comments (REQ-008 from CC-0007). This prevents supply-chain attacks via tag mutation and is validated by the existing test_actions_pinned_to_sha and test_actions_have_version_comments tests.

scenarios.name: metadata-action pinned to SHA
scenarios.when: the workflow file is parsed for action references
and_then: the existing test_actions_pinned_to_sha test passes without modification

scenarios.name: metadata-action has version comment
scenarios.when: the workflow file is parsed for action references
scenarios.then: all docker/metadata-action lines end with # v5
and_then: the existing test_actions_have_version_comments test passes without modification

scenarios.name: SHA corresponds to correct version tag
scenarios.when: the pinned SHA is verified against the docker/metadata-action GitHub repository
scenarios.then: the SHA resolves to the v5 release tag
and_then: no version mismatch between the SHA and the comment

requirements.description: All generated and static labels SHALL use the org.opencontainers.image.* annotation key namespace as defined by the OCI Image Spec
requirements.rationale: Using the standard OCI annotation namespace ensures labels are recognized by container registries (Docker Hub, GHCR, Quay), vulnerability scanners, and audit tools. Non-standard keys would not be displayed or indexed by these tools.

scenarios.name: Static Dockerfile labels use OCI namespace
scenarios.when: the Dockerfiles are inspected for LABEL instructions
scenarios.then: all label keys start with org.opencontainers.image.
and_then: no custom or vendor-specific label keys are used

scenarios.name: metadata-action labels use OCI namespace
scenarios.when: the metadata-action labels input is inspected
scenarios.then: all custom label keys start with org.opencontainers.image.
and_then: auto-generated labels (created, revision, source, url, version) also use the OCI namespace by default

scenarios.name: Complete set of OCI keys present on pushed images
scenarios.then: the image carries all nine specified annotations: title, description, source, url, version, created, revision, licenses, vendor
and_then: no labels from the specified set are missing

requirements.description: Each metadata-action step's labels output SHALL be wired into the corresponding build-push-action step via the labels input
requirements.rationale: docker/build-push-action's labels input applies OCI annotations to the built image manifest. Without wiring, the metadata-action output would be generated but never applied to the image.

scenarios.name: python-base build-push-action receives labels
scenarios.when: the build-python-base step is configured in the workflow
and_then: the labels are applied to the pushed python-base image manifest

scenarios.name: venv-builder build-push-action receives labels
scenarios.when: the build-venv-builder step is configured in the workflow
and_then: the labels are applied to the pushed venv-builder image manifest

scenarios.name: keystone build-push-action receives labels
scenarios.when: the build-service step is configured in the workflow
and_then: the labels are applied to the pushed keystone image manifest

tasks.title: Add static OCI LABEL instructions to python-base, venv-builder, and keystone Dockerfiles (CC-0031, REQ-001, REQ-005)
Add docker/metadata-action steps for python-base and venv-builder in build-base-images job and wire labels output into build-push-action steps (CC-0031, REQ-002, REQ-004, REQ-005, REQ-006)

Add docker/metadata-action step for keystone in build-service-images job with raw version strategy overriding version to steps.source-ref.outputs.ref, and wire labels into build-service step (CC-0031, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-005, REQ-006)

Add CC-0031 verification tests to verify_build_images_workflow.sh: metadata-action step existence, labels wiring to build-push-action, keystone raw version strategy, OCI label keys in metadata-action, and static Dockerfile LABEL presence (CC-0031, REQ-001 through REQ-006)

Update docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md with OCI Annotations section documenting metadata-action steps, labels wiring, and keystone version override; update docs/reference/container-images.md Dockerfile sections with static LABEL documentation (CC-0031)

test_specifications.story: DevOps engineer inspects image provenance in container registry

test_specifications.story: CI pipeline stamps keystone image with upstream release version

test_specifications.story: CI pipeline stamps keystone image with upstream release version
test_specifications.expected: meta-python-base and meta-venv-builder steps do not specify a tags input (use default Git-derived version strategy)

test_specifications.story: DevOps engineer inspects image provenance in container registry
DevOps engineer inspects image provenance in container registry
DevOps engineer inspects image provenance in container registry
Security auditor verifies supply chain metadata on container images
test_specifications.expected: All 3 metadata-action steps include org.opencontainers.image.title in their labels input

test_specifications.story: Security auditor verifies supply chain metadata on container images

test_specifications.story: Security auditor verifies supply chain metadata on container images
test_specifications.expected: All 3 metadata-action steps include org.opencontainers.image.vendor in their labels input

test_specifications.story: Developer identifies locally-built images via static labels
test_specifications.expected: images/python-base/Dockerfile contains LABEL instructions for org.opencontainers.image.title, .description, .licenses, .vendor

test_specifications.story: Developer identifies locally-built images via static labels
test_specifications.expected: images/venv-builder/Dockerfile contains LABEL instructions for org.opencontainers.image.title, .description, .licenses, .vendor

test_specifications.story: Developer identifies locally-built images via static labels
test_specifications.expected: images/keystone/Dockerfile contains LABEL instructions for org.opencontainers.image.title, .description, .licenses, .vendor in the runtime stage (Stage 2)

review_criteria: All docker/metadata-action uses are pinned to a 40-character SHA with # v5 comment — existing test_actions_pinned_to_sha and test_actions_have_version_comments pass without modification
All three Dockerfiles contain static LABEL instructions for org.opencontainers.image.title, .description, .licenses=Apache-2.0, .vendor — with labels in keystone's Stage 2 (runtime) only
All three build-push-action steps (build-python-base, build-venv-builder, build-service) have labels: ${{ steps.<meta-step-id>.outputs.labels }} wiring
The meta-keystone step uses type=raw,value=${{ steps.source-ref.outputs.ref }} for version override; meta-python-base and meta-venv-builder do not override version
Existing verify_build_images_workflow.sh tests pass without modification (no regressions in CC-0007/CC-0029 tests)
New CC-0031 test functions validate: metadata-action step existence, labels wiring, keystone version strategy, OCI label keys, and static Dockerfile LABEL presence
No changes to existing tag derivation logic, verify job structure, SBOM/attestation configuration, or Makefile
Reference documentation in docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md and docs/reference/container-images.md accurately describes the two-layer OCI annotation approach
implementation_notes: Two-layer approach: static LABEL instructions in Dockerfiles provide baseline metadata for local builds (title, description, licenses, vendor), while docker/metadata-action in CI generates dynamic labels (created, revision, source, url, version) that supplement static ones at push time. The metadata-action step must be placed BEFORE its corresponding build-push-action step in each job. For base images, metadata-action steps go after the GHCR login step but before each build-push-action step. For keystone, the meta-keystone step goes after the Derive tags step (needs steps.tags.outputs.image) and after source-ref (needs steps.source-ref.outputs.ref), but before build-service. The version override for keystone uses the raw tag strategy: tags: type=raw,value=${{ steps.source-ref.outputs.ref }}. Static Dockerfile labels: in python-base and venv-builder, add LABEL after the last RUN instruction. In keystone, add LABEL in Stage 2 (runtime, FROM python-base) after the USER instruction — Stage 1 (build) labels are discarded by Docker. The docker/metadata-action v5 SHA must be looked up from https://github.com/docker/metadata-action/tags at implementation time and pinned in the same format as other actions (40-char SHA # v5). The metadata-action images input should use ghcr.io/${{ steps.meta.outputs.owner }}/<image-name> for base images (reusing the existing meta step that lowercases the owner) and ${{ steps.tags.outputs.image }} for keystone (reusing the existing tags step output). The labels input in each metadata-action step provides the four custom labels (title, description, licenses, vendor); the action auto-generates created, revision, source, url, and version from the GitHub context.
